text:
3. Join Trellis reconciliation events to NMP kernel outcomes. NMP already applies Trellis ResourcePlan as ApplyDependentInterestDelta; now it needs to retain/export bounded diagnostic
     records instead of test-only traces. [...] ## ADR-0075 current stance
`docs/decisions/0075-trellis-private-reconciliation-substrate.md` in nostr-multi-platform. Decision: NMP may use Trellis as *private* reconciliation machinery below typed read sessions ("Trellis owns generic mechanics. NMP owns Nostr and product meaning."). Draws one boundary: Trellis primitives must never become app/native/web-facing or appear in builder docs; NMP owns resource identity semantics even where Trellis owns identity mechanics. "Boundaries" forbids "exported Trellis types in app/native/web APIs" with no carve-out for a diagnostic/tooling surface; "Enforcement" says doctrine tests/public API checks reject raw Trellis primitives in "app/native/web-facing NMP surfaces" — silent on any dev/diagnostic surface. Does not currently mention `nmp-devtools`, receipts, or diagnostics at all. This is exactly the gap #2809 proposes to close by adding a second, dev-build-only "diagnostic surface" category. Related links: ADR-0070 (typed read sessions), ADR-0076 (app-facing feed helpers), epics #2626/#2627 (both closed). [...] - **Phase B — nmp-devtools stream**: ordered open/replace/refresh/close events with owner/provenance/revision/refcount/teardown data; join Trellis `ResourcePlan` → `ApplyDependentInterestDelta` outcomes into bounded retained records (today they're test-only traces); correlate with the existing relay diagnostics; and define the **NMP-owned receipt shape** so Chirp never sees `ResourcePlan`/`ResourceKey`/`Graph`. [...] Assistant: **What the amendment is:** ADR-0075 is the decision that lets NMP use Trellis internally ("private reconciliation substrate") — and its Boundaries section flatly forbids Trellis types in *any* app/native/web-facing API, enforced by doctrine-lint and the leak ratchet. That rule has no carve-out, so `nmp-devtools` — which by definition exposes Trellis trace/audit receipts to tooling — would be a doctrine violation the day it's written. The amendment (#2809) adds a second category to the ADR: a **diagnostic surface** — dev-build-only, never linked by app code, compiled out of release artifacts — where Trellis vocabulary may legally appear. Without it, every phase of the X-Ray epic is fighting the linter; with it, the lint gate knows exactly what's exempt. [...] **What it is:** ADR-0075 is the ruling that lets NMP use Trellis as its private reconciliation substrate, with a hard boundary: no Trellis vocabulary in any app/native/web-facing surface, enforced by doctrine-lint. The amendment (#2809) adds a second surface category — a **diagnostic surface**: a dev-build-only `nmp-devtools` crate that *may* see Trellis trace/audit data, is never linked by app code, and is compiled out of release builds. Without it, the entire X-Ray epic would be a standing lint violation; with it, the exemption is explicit and machine-checkable.
